야고보서 2:1.content: My brothers, don’t hold the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ of glory with partiality.

야고보서 2:2.content: For if a man with a gold ring, in fine clothing, comes into your synagogue, and a poor man in filthy clothing also comes in,

야고보서 2:3.content: and you pay special attention to him who wears the fine clothing and say, “Sit here in a good place;” and you tell the poor man, “Stand there,” or “Sit by my footstool”

야고보서 2:4.content: haven’t you shown partiality among yourselves, and become judges with evil thoughts?

야고보서 2:5.content: Listen, my beloved brothers. Didn’t God choose those who are poor in this world to be rich in faith, and heirs of the Kingdom which he promised to those who love him?

야고보서 2:6.content: But you have dishonored the poor man. Don’t the rich oppress you, and personally drag you before the courts?

야고보서 2:7.content: Don’t they blaspheme the honorable name by which you are called?

야고보서 2:8.content: However, if you fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.

야고보서 2:9.content: But if you show partiality, you commit sin, being convicted by the law as transgressors.

야고보서 2:10.content: For whoever keeps the whole law, and yet stumbles in one point, he has become guilty of all.

야고보서 2:11.content: For he who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not commit murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.

야고보서 2:12.content: So speak and so do, as men who are to be judged by a law of freedom.

야고보서 2:13.content: For judgment is without mercy to him who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.

야고보서 2:14.content: What good is it, my brothers, if a man says he has faith, but has no works? Can faith save him?

야고보서 2:15.content: And if a brother or sister is naked and in lack of daily food,

야고보서 2:16.content: and one of you tells them, “Go in peace. Be warmed and filled;” yet you didn’t give them the things the body needs, what good is it?

야고보서 2:17.content: Even so faith, if it has no works, is dead in itself.

야고보서 2:18.content: Yes, a man will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I will show you my faith by my works.

야고보서 2:19.content: You believe that God is one. You do well. The demons also believe, and shudder.

야고보서 2:20.content: But do you want to know, vain man, that faith apart from works is dead?

야고보서 2:21.content: Wasn’t Abraham our father justified by works, in that he offered up Isaac his son on the altar?

야고보서 2:22.content: You see that faith worked with his works, and by works faith was perfected.

야고보서 2:23.content: So the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him as righteousness,” and he was called the friend of God.

야고보서 2:24.content: You see then that by works, a man is justified, and not only by faith.

야고보서 2:25.content: In the same way, wasn’t Rahab the prostitute also justified by works, in that she received the messengers and sent them out another way?

야고보서 2:26.content: For as the body apart from the spirit is dead, even so faith apart from works is dead.
